text.title: nginx Reverse Proxy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the nginx Reverse Proxy methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: When stripping a URL prefix, use `rewrite ^/prefix/(.*) /$1 break; proxy_pass http://upstream;` — NEVER `proxy_pass http://upstream/;` with a trailing slash on multi-segment locations.

rationale.source: nginx core docs + multi-year regression cohort

rule.rationale: Trailing-slash proxy_pass collapses to URI-rewrite semantics with edge cases on multi-segment paths; explicit rewrite+break is deterministic.

rule.statement: Declare `map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade { default upgrade; '' close; }` in the http context; reference `$connection_upgrade` in WebSocket locations via a snippet — never inline.
rule.rationale: The map MUST live in http; inlining the literal `Connection upgrade` header drops requests when the client sends `Upgrade: ''`.

rule.statement: All proxy locations set `proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;` when Cloudflare full(strict) terminates TLS — never echo $scheme.

rationale.source: Cloudflare proxy semantics + Django SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER docs

rule.rationale: Cloudflare sends HTTP to origin; $scheme is `http` and breaks Django CSRF, FastAPI redirects, secure-cookie issuance.

rule.statement: Content-Security-Policy is declared per-vhost — never in a global snippet shared across apps.
rule.rationale: Each app has its own allow-list; a shared CSP either blocks functionality or is too permissive to matter.

rule.statement: `limit_req_zone` lives in http context; `limit_req zone=... burst=...` lives in the location — declaring the zone inside a server block is rejected by nginx -t.
rule.rationale: nginx parses zones once globally; per-server declarations either silently inherit or fail config-test.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-nginx-reverse-proxy.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
